about.bio: I design and build AI-powered digital products — through Analytica Studio, I help companies worldwide automate workflows and implement intelligent solutions. My edge: 3+ years of enterprise BPMN process modeling combined with hands-on AI implementation using Claude API, GPT-4, n8n, and Make.com. By day, I'm a Product Owner at Tietoevry working on healthcare systems (openEHR). Based in Kraków, available remote worldwide — in Polish, English, Norwegian, and Spanish.
about.pillar1Desc: CPRE-certified. 3+ years BPMN in enterprise healthcare. I design the process first, then build the AI layer on top.
about.pillar2Title: I build with Claude, GPT-4, n8n & Make.com
about.pillar2Desc: From chatbots to full workflow automations. Next.js, React, TypeScript for the frontend.
about.pillar3Desc: Polish, English, Norwegian (medical interpreter), Spanish (B2). Based in Kraków. Available remote worldwide.
about.build1Desc: Custom AI assistants and copilots trained on your business data and processes — integrated into your existing tools or products.
about.build2Desc: Fast, modern websites and full web applications — from landing pages and portfolios to multi-page products with CMS and auth.
about.build3Desc: End-to-end automation of repetitive business processes — connecting your apps, data, and AI models without the manual work.
about.build4Desc: Process analysis and redesign that makes your workflows ready for AI implementation — from AS-IS mapping to TO-BE automation blueprint.
about.role1Title: System Analyst → Sr. System Analyst → Product Owner · Tietoevry
about.role1Desc: 3+ years in healthcare IT (openEHR). Grew from System Analyst to Product Owner. BPMN process modeling (AS-IS/TO-BE), requirements engineering (CPRE), Figma wireframes, user stories, UML, Scrum Master, UX research, product roadmap and backlog management, cross-functional team collaboration.
about.role2Desc: Next.js websites, n8n & Make.com automations, AI chatbots, BPMN consulting. Clients worldwide, based in Kraków.
about.role3Title: Financial Analyst · BMI Group & Tate & Lyle
about.role3Desc: Financial reporting automation: Power Query, SAP, SQL.
about.role4Title: AR Analyst & Backup Team Leader · IBM
about.role4Desc: Nordic markets (Norway, Denmark, Sweden), AR/AP management, recruitment backup.
about.certMore: ...and more in Blockchain, SQL, and Agile
about.edu1Desc: BPMN modeling, process automation, ERP, ITIL framework.

about.edu3Desc: Programming (HTML, Java), Linux, Cybersecurity, UX, Databases.
about.bookDesc: Chapter on blockchain technology's impact on business processes and stakeholders. Published in Poland, 2024.

make-reporting.overview: An e-commerce company was generating reports manually — an analyst would export data from several systems (Google Sheets, Xero, Airtable, WMS), merge them in Excel and send them by email each week. The process took 4–6 hours per week and was prone to errors. I designed and implemented an automated reporting system in Make.com that pulls data from 5 sources, processes it and delivers reports to the right recipients — with zero human intervention.
make-reporting.processTitle: How I built the reporting system
process: Process audit — mapped all data sources, report schedule (daily, weekly, monthly) and the full list of recipients and output formats (PDF, XLSX, Slack, Notion).
Make.com architecture — designed 4 independent scenarios: daily sales report, weekly finance summary, ops KPI dashboard and inventory alert.
Data integrations — connections to Google Sheets (sales), Xero (finance), Airtable (operations), WMS API (warehouse) and HubSpot CRM (pipeline).
Formatting & distribution — automatic PDF and XLSX generation, delivery to management inboxes, live dashboard published to Notion, Slack notifications to the relevant channels.
Monitoring & alerts — automatic notifications on connection errors, retry logic, execution logs and a monthly system health-check report.
make-reporting.result: 4–6 hours of manual work per week → 0 minutes. 12 reports generated automatically every month. Report delivery time cut from 48 hours to 8 minutes. Data errors reduced by 94% thanks to input validation.

bpmn-logistyka.overview: LogiFlow Sp. z o.o. — a logistics company handling 400+ orders per day — was struggling with a chaotic, fully manual order fulfilment process. Orders arrived by phone and email, credit checks took 2 days, warehouse picking had an 8% error rate, and invoices were issued manually after 48 hours. I conducted a full BPMN process analysis and implemented 9 automations.
process: Phase 1 – AS-IS mapping (2 wks): 12 interviews with employees (sales, warehouse, logistics, finance), process observation and current-state mapping in BPMN 2.0 using Visio Professional, identifying 5 critical bottlenecks.
Phase 2 – Analysis & prioritisation (1 wk): Time-cost analysis of each step, ROI-difficulty prioritisation matrix, workshops with management.
Phase 3 – TO-BE design (1 wk): Optimised process diagram with 9 automations — B2B portal, auto credit check via BIK API (3 sec), WMS auto-allocation, AI Route Planner, mobile e-POD, auto-invoicing.
Phase 4 – Iterative implementation (3 sprints × 2 wks): Automations rolled out in ROI order, parallel testing (new vs old process), employee training.
Phase 5 – Monitoring & report: Analytics dashboard with KPIs, 30-day results review.
bpmn-logistyka.result: Order fulfilment time: 3.2 days → 4.5 hours (↓94%). Picking errors: 8.2% → 0.3% (↓96%). 312 working hours saved per month (≈€9,360). Project ROI: 8 months. Full elimination of paper-based document flow.

ai-onboarding.overview: A SaaS agency onboarding dozens of new clients every month was spending an average of 17 minutes per client on manual tasks — writing welcome emails, creating Notion task lists, updating the CRM and notifying the team on Slack. I built a fully automated pipeline using n8n and the Claude API that completes the entire process in 3–4 seconds.
process: Process audit — mapped every step of the manual onboarding flow and identified bottlenecks: copy-paste errors, inconsistent emails, delays between steps.
Workflow design in n8n — Webhook trigger → data validation → CRM check (HubSpot) → Claude AI → parallel output actions.
Prompt engineering for Claude — designed a system prompt that generates a personalised welcome email (PL/EN), a 12-item onboarding task list and a CRM note from raw client data.
Integrations — Gmail/SendGrid (email), Notion (tasks), HubSpot (deal stage → 'Onboarded'), Slack (notification to #onboarding channel).
Testing & monitoring — analytics dashboard, automatic error alerts and retry logic for failed nodes.
ai-onboarding.result: Onboarding time reduced from 17 minutes to 3.4 seconds — an 80% reduction. The agency handled 127 clients in the first month with zero manual intervention. Savings: ~312 working hours per month.

stork.overview: Stork Wykończenia is a Kraków-based company specialising in apartment and house finishing — from bathrooms, through living rooms, to terraces and office spaces. The client needed an elegant, professional website that reflects the premium nature of his services and lets potential clients instantly estimate renovation costs.
process: Client discovery call — goals, target audience, visual style and scope of services.
Visual concept: dark premium theme, gold accents, characterful typography.
Sections built: hero with stats, about, services, filterable project gallery (bathrooms, living rooms, terraces, offices), reviews, pricing and contact.
Interactive renovation cost calculator — clients choose floor area, number of rooms, finishing standard and property type; the page calculates the estimated cost in real time.
Scroll animations, full mobile responsiveness and final delivery.
stork.result: Website completed in 2 days. The client received a modern, fast and elegant online presence with a built-in quote calculator — a tool that actively helps him generate new enquiries.

craft-cafe.overview: Craft Café is a unique café in Kraków's Kazimierz district, combining great coffee with ceramics workshops and local events. The client needed a website that captures the atmosphere of the place, enables online table reservations and workshop sign-ups.
process: Meeting with the client — discussing the café's character, values, aesthetic and functional needs.
Visual design inspired by Kazimierz: warm tones, organic shapes, elegant typography.
Sections built: hero, about, menu, ceramics workshops with sign-ups, events calendar and table reservation.
Interactive calendar and reservation form — no third-party services.
Cross-device testing and final delivery.
craft-cafe.result: The website reflects the unique character of Craft Café and allows customers to easily book tables and sign up for workshops — all in one place.

prices.subtitle: Choose the package that fits your needs
prices.monthlyBanner: Monthly care from 200 PLN/month — hosting, updates, support
prices.faq1Q: How long does a project take?
prices.faq1A: Depends on the scope. Simple website — 1-2 weeks. AI app with automation — 3-6 weeks. Detailed timeline is agreed upon in the first meeting.
prices.faq2A: Yes! Monthly care includes hosting, updates, bugfixes, and basic technical support.
prices.faq3A: I work with 50% upfront payment before starting, and the remaining 50% after completion. I accept bank transfers.
prices.faq4Q: Do you work with international clients?
prices.faq4A: Yes! I work with clients from Poland, Norway, and across Europe. All projects are done remotely.
prices.faq5Q: What if I have special requirements?
prices.faq5A: Each project is customized individually. Contact me, and we'll discuss your needs and find the best solution.
prices.metaDesc: Clear pricing for websites, AI apps, and automation.
